content.full_text:
DEC-12-91 THU 15:49     SUTTER COUNTY          FAX NO. 9167417103          P.02


                          OFFICE OF THE COUNTY COUNSEL
                               COUNTY OF SUTTER              RONALD S. ERICKSON
                                                                Assistant
DARRELL W. LARSEN         1160 Civic Center Boulevard
  County Counsel          Yuba City, California 95993      JAMES SCANLON
                                                            JOSEPH P. CERULLO
                          Telephone (916) 741-7110
                                                            DONALD F. STANTON
                                                               Deputies

                               December 12, 1991


     Fair Political Practices
     Commission
     428 J Street, Suite 800
     P.O. Box 807
     Sacramento, CA 95804

     ATTN.:  Jonathan Rothman, Legal Division

     Dear Mr. Rothman:

     As we discussed via telephone on December 11, 1991, I am
requesting, on behalf of Sutter County Supervisor Joe Benatar,
formal written advice pursuant to Government Code section 83114(b)
as well as Title 2 California Code of Regulations section 18329(b).
I have been authorized by Mr. Benatar to make this inquiry.  His
mailing address is 1160 Civic Center Boulevard, Yuba City,
California 95993.   Mr. Benatar's inquiry is whether he may
participate in consideration by the Sutter County Board of
Supervisors of a proposed general plan amendment and associated EIR
for South Sutter County.    This matter is scheduled for
consideration by the Board on December 17, 1991.  The proposed
general plan amendment concerns land use of approximately 24,715
acres of what is now predominantly agricultural ground south of the
cross canal in Sutter County.   The general plan amendment proposes
that 9,331 acres of the subject property be devoted to residential
uses, 4,672 acres be devoted to commercial uses, provision be made
for construction of 57,522 residences resulting in an increased
population for the area of 142,653 persons.  Mr. Benatar is an
employee of the Fidelity National Title Company.    Fidelity has a
division serving Sutter, Yuba, and Plumas Counties of which
division Mr. Benatar is the president.     He has branch managers
dealing with the day-to-day business of the company in each of the
three counties.   Mr. Benatar does share in company bonuses in turn
based on corporate profits.   Fidelity National Title Company has
offices in 33 states.   Its stock is traded on the American Stock
Exchange.  Mr. Benatar through the company's retirement program, is
the owner of stock in Fidelity National Title.    He believes his
stock holdings are currently valued at less than $10,000.     Mr.
Benatar's company has done business, in the past, with
landowners owning properties in the South Sutter County area,
including some large landowners.     Those landowners, in turn, have
done business with other title companies in Sutter County as well
as with Fidelity.

DEC-12-91 THU 15:50    SUTTER COUNTY         FAX NO. 9167417103      P.03

Fair Political Practices
Commission
December 12, 1991
Page 2

The area addressed in the proposed general plan amendment was the
subject of two ballot measures addressed by county voters in a May
1991 election. The first measure, which would have applied only to
South Sutter County, fixed land uses as they are designated in the
1990 General Plan for the area. Changes could have occurred only
as a consequence of another initiative measure. The initiative
specifically prohibited the designation of "new towns" in the South
Sutter County area.

The other initiative would have been applicable to the entire
unincorporated area, would have permitted the Board of Supervisors
to amend land uses shown in the then existing general plan upon a
unanimous vote and stated that it was inconsistent with and
intended to be an alternative to the first measure discussed above.
Both measures failed and processing of the South Sutter County
general plan amendment which is the subject of Mr. Benatar's
inquiry, continued. As mentioned, it is now ready for
consideration by the Board of Supervisors.

Finally, as mentioned to you in our telephone conversation, the
subject of Mr. Benatar's participation in Sutter County land use
determinations is one which has been examined by the enforcement
division of the Commission. Attached hereto please find a May 16,
1983 letter addressed to Supervisor Benatar from the director of
the enforcement division concluding that no violation occurred as
a consequence of Mr. Benatar's participation in an earlier decision
and setting forth a general outline of issues examined as well as
the rationale applied by FPPC staff at that time.

Should you need additional information, please do not hesitate to
call upon us.

Very truly yours,

DARRELL W. LARSEN
SUTTER COUNTY COUNSEL

DWL:js

Enclosure

cc: Supervisor Joe Benatar
